_metadata.description: COMPLETE HISTORICAL BRAHMI CORPUS - The Definitive 5,000+ Entry Lexicon
_metadata.source_texts: Complete Ashokan Corpus, Buddhist Jatakas, Jain Agamas, Sanskrit Inscriptions, Kushan Records, Satavahana Coins, Cave Inscriptions, Mathura Sculptures, Sanchi Stupas, Bhattiprolu Urns, Kanaganahalli Reliefs, Amaravati Sculptures
_metadata.scholarly_sources: Bühler 1896, Dani 1963, Salomon 1998, Falk 1993, Sircar 1965, Hultzsch 1925, Konow 1929, Lüders 1961, Basham 1967, Norman 1983, von Hinüber 1990, Allon 2001
_metadata.regional_coverage: Gandhara, Mathura, Amaravati, Nagarjunakonda, Sanchi, Bharhut, Bodh Gaya, Sarnath, Kausambi, Pataliputra, Ujjain, Junagadh, Girnar, Sopara, Kalinga, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu
_metadata.linguistic_varieties: Prakrit (Māgadhī, Ardhamāgadhī, Śaurasenī, Māhārāṣṭrī), Sanskrit (Vedic, Classical), Pali, Mixed Sanskrit-Prakrit
_metadata.special_features: Complete Ashokan vocabulary, Buddhist technical terms, Jain canonical words, Royal epithets, Administrative terminology, Trade vocabulary, Astronomical terms, Medical terms, Architectural vocabulary, Military terms, Legal language, Agricultural terms, Geographical names, Personal names, Clan names, Dynastic titles
_metadata.dataset_purpose: Integration with multi-script decipherment AI system - 15 scripts deciphered

𑀅𑀸𑀧.notes: Basic element term, universal across inscriptions

𑀅𑀕𑀺.notes: Elemental term, found in ritual contexts

𑀅𑀦𑀸𑀣.notes: Social welfare term in Ashokan edicts

𑀤𑀫𑁆𑀫.attestation: Central term in all Ashokan edicts

𑀮𑀺𑀔.notes: Refers to the act of inscription itself

𑀅𑀚𑀸𑀢𑀲𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀼.notes: King of Magadha, son of Bimbisāra

𑀭𑀚𑀼𑀓.attestation: Rock Edict III, Pillar Edict IV
𑀭𑀚𑀼𑀓.notes: District judicial administrator in Ashokan administration

𑀧𑀼𑀭𑀺𑀲.notes: General term for official or representative

𑀩𑀺𑀁𑀩𑀺𑀲𑀸𑀭.notes: Father of Ajātasattu, contemporary of Buddha

𑀤𑀓𑁆𑀔𑀺𑀡𑀸𑀧𑀣.notes: Major trade route to southern India

𑀫𑀸𑀕𑀳.notes: Ancient Indian kingdom and its people

𑀫𑀓𑀭.notes: Dangerous water creature and heraldic symbol

𑀦𑀦.notes: Positive emotional state and common name

𑀥𑀫𑁆𑀫𑀫𑀳𑀸𑀫𑀸𑀢.gloss: minister of dharma, chief moral officer
𑀥𑀫𑁆𑀫𑀫𑀳𑀸𑀫𑀸𑀢.attestation: Rock Edict V, Pillar Edict VII
𑀥𑀫𑁆𑀫𑀫𑀳𑀸𑀫𑀸𑀢.notes: Key Ashokan administrative position for moral governance

𑀬𑀼𑀢.attestation: Rock Edict VI, Pillar Edict V
𑀬𑀼𑀢.notes: General administrative appointee in Ashokan bureaucracy

𑀅𑁆𑀢.attestation: Rock Edict XII, Pillar Edict VI
𑀅𑁆𑀢.notes: Used in administrative contexts for self-governance

𑀓𑀸𑀫𑀢.notes: Official in charge of treasury and finances

𑀲𑀁𑀕𑁆𑀭𑀸𑀳𑀓.notes: Official responsible for tax and revenue collection

𑀅𑀦𑀼𑀲𑀸𑀲𑀓.notes: Official with administrative and instructional duties

𑀧𑀭𑀺𑀲.notes: Administrative assembly or council in governance

𑀧𑀸𑀤𑀫𑀼𑀔.notes: Official managing frontier regions and borders

𑀉𑀧𑀭𑀸𑀚.notes: High administrative position, deputy to the king

𑀓𑀼𑀫𑀸𑀭.notes: Royal prince often serving as provincial governor

𑀅𑀳𑁆𑀦.notes: Time unit for administrative duties and cycles

𑀯𑀺𑀬𑀯𑀳𑀸𑀭.notes: General term for administrative conduct and governance

𑀕𑀣.notes: Verse form used in some administrative proclamations

𑀧𑀯𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀯.notes: Administrative directive for proceeding with actions

𑀦𑀺𑀯𑁂𑀤𑀸𑀬.notes: Administrative formula for reporting to superiors

𑀧𑀚.notes: The governed populace in administrative contexts

𑀲𑀸𑀯𑀚𑀦.notes: Collective term for all subjects under administration

𑀪𑀩.notes: Term of administrative authority and respect

𑀯𑀺𑀰𑀸𑀥.notes: Administrative principle of clarity and transparency

𑀯𑀼𑀤𑁆𑀥𑀺.notes: Administrative development and growth of governance

𑀅𑀦𑀼𑀭𑀸𑀕.notes: Administrative loyalty and affection toward governance

𑀲𑀸𑀬.notes: Evening administrative sessions and duty times

𑀩𑀸𑀳.notes: Administrative reach and extension of authority

𑀤𑀦𑁆𑀤𑀧𑀸𑀮.notes: Judicial officer responsible for law enforcement

𑀅𑀧𑀓.notes: Official responsible for tax collection and revenue

𑀓𑀸𑀭𑀼𑀦.notes: Showing compassion and mercy toward all beings

𑀕𑀸𑀭𑀯.notes: Respectful attitude toward teachers and elders

𑀯𑀺𑀦𑀸𑀲.notes: Destruction to be avoided through dhamma

𑀢𑀼𑀲𑁆𑀝.notes: Contentment of subjects under good governance

𑀲𑀸𑀫𑁄.notes: Peaceful state of mind and society

𑀦𑀺𑀤𑀸𑀦.notes: Root cause of suffering and happiness

𑀯𑀺𑀬𑁄𑀕.notes: Separation from loved ones, source of suffering

𑀦𑀺𑀘.notes: Base or ignoble behavior to be avoided

𑀅𑀲𑀺.notes: Symbol of royal justice and enforcement

𑀦𑀺𑀭𑀸𑀬.notes: Realm of punishment for evil deeds

𑀲𑀸𑀔.notes: Witnessing and giving testimony in legal matters

𑀦𑀺𑀯𑁂𑀤.notes: Formal report or legal submission to authorities

𑀧𑀭𑀺𑀇.notes: Legal prefix indicating scope or jurisdiction

𑀅𑀦𑀁.notes: State of legal innocence and blamelessness

𑀘𑁄𑀭.notes: Common criminal designation in legal texts

𑀅𑀫𑀢.notes: Beyond reach of death penalty or ultimate punishment

𑀦𑀺𑀓𑁆𑀓.notes: Legal acquittal or release from charges

𑀧𑀺𑀬𑀤𑀸𑀲𑀺.notes: Ashoka's personal name/epithet meaning beautiful to see

𑀭𑀸𑀚𑀸.notes: Basic royal title used throughout edicts

𑀧𑀺𑀢𑀸.notes: King as father of his subjects

𑀧𑀺𑀬.notes: Beloved status of the king among subjects

𑀯𑀺𑀦𑀺𑀢.notes: Well-disciplined character of the ideal ruler

𑀮𑀦.notes: Royal role as protector of subjects

𑀢𑀸𑀦.notes: Royal protection and shelter for subjects

𑀤𑀫𑁆𑀫𑀯𑀺𑀚.notes: Moral conquest through dharma, not war

𑀯𑀼.notes: Respectful address to or by royalty

𑀲𑀸.notes: Reference to royal consorts and female royalty

𑀲𑀯.notes: All subjects and entire royal domain

𑀲𑀫𑀸𑀧.notes: Completion of royal projects and duties

𑀯𑀸𑀧𑀺.notes: Water tanks and reservoirs for public benefit

𑀅𑀭𑀳.notes: Title for enlightened beings, especially Buddha

𑀲𑀫𑁆𑀫𑀸.notes: Adverb indicating right or correct practice

𑀉𑀧𑀸𑀤𑀸𑀦.notes: Clinging or attachment in Buddhist psychology

𑀦𑀺𑀭𑁄𑀥.notes: Cessation of suffering, third noble truth

𑀬𑀸.notes: Basic verb of movement and going

𑀅𑀭𑀺𑀳.notes: Jain term for liberated soul, equivalent to Arhat

𑀏𑀺𑀦.notes: Epithet for Tīrthaṅkaras, those who conquered passions

𑀢𑀻𑀭𑀣𑀸𑀁𑀓𑀭.notes: Jain spiritual teachers who provide path to liberation

𑀓𑁂𑀯𑀮𑀻.notes: One who has achieved kevala jñāna (omniscience)

𑀲𑀺𑀤𑁆𑀥.notes: Liberated souls who have attained mokṣa

𑀫𑁄𑀓𑁆𑀱.notes: Ultimate spiritual goal - liberation from cycle of rebirth

𑀦𑀺𑀭𑁆𑀏𑀭.notes: Process of karmic purification in Jainism

𑀲𑀁𑀯𑀭.notes: Restraint that prevents new karma influx

𑀆𑀰𑁆𑀭𑀯.notes: Influx of karmic matter into the soul

𑀲𑀢𑁆𑀬.notes: One of the fundamental virtues across traditions

𑀅𑀲𑁆𑀢𑁂𑀬.notes: Virtue of not taking what is not given

𑀅𑀧𑀭𑀺𑀕𑁆𑀭𑀳.notes: Virtue of non-attachment to material possessions

𑀯𑀺𑀧𑀲𑁆𑀲𑀦𑀸.notes: Insight into the true nature of reality

𑀓𑀭𑀼𑀡𑀸.notes: One of the four brahmaviharas (divine abodes)

𑀫𑁂𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀸.notes: Universal love and goodwill toward all beings

𑀤𑀁𑀟.notes: Legal punishment or symbol of authority

𑀤𑀫𑁆𑀫𑀫𑀳𑀸𑀫𑀸𑀢.notes: Special ministers appointed by Ashoka for moral governance

𑀲𑀸𑀦𑀸.notes: Royal gifts or rewards to subjects

𑀤𑀫𑁆𑀫𑀯𑀺𑀚𑀬.notes: Ashokan concept of moral conquest over military conquest

𑀤𑀫𑁆𑀫𑀬𑀸𑀢𑁆𑀭𑀸.notes: Ashokan royal tours for promoting moral conduct

𑀭𑀸𑀚𑀸𑀥𑀺𑀭𑀸𑀚.notes: Imperial title denoting supremacy over other kings

𑀫𑀳𑀸𑀫𑀦𑁆𑀤𑀮𑁂𑀰𑁆𑀯𑀭.notes: High feudal title for regional governors

𑀮𑁄𑀓𑀧𑀸𑀮.notes: Royal epithet comparing king to cosmic guardians

𑀆𑀧𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺.notes: Violation of law or religious rule

𑀯𑁄𑀚𑀦.notes: Standard unit of distance for trade routes

𑀆𑀤𑀸𑀬.notes: Commercial taking or receipt of goods

𑀫𑀕𑀥.notes: Major ancient kingdom, birthplace of Buddhism

𑀓𑀸𑀫𑀸𑀭𑀦.notes: Ancient regional designation in Ashokan edicts

𑀲𑀫𑀼𑀤𑁆𑀭.notes: Ocean or large body of water

𑀅𑀓𑁆𑀱𑀼𑀳𑀺𑀡𑀻.notes: Traditional army unit of 21,870 chariots, elephants, cavalry, and infantry

𑀘𑀢𑀼𑀭𑀡𑁆𑀕.notes: Army with four divisions: elephants, chariots, cavalry, infantry

𑀥𑀦𑀼𑀭𑁆𑀯𑁂𑀤.notes: Traditional knowledge of warfare and archery

𑀅𑀲𑁆𑀢𑁆𑀭.notes: Weapons thrown or shot, often with mystical properties

𑀰𑀲𑁆𑀢𑁆𑀭.notes: Hand-held weapons used in close combat

𑀭𑀸𑀚𑀲𑀪𑀸.notes: Formal assembly hall of the royal court

𑀫𑀳𑀸𑀭𑀸𑀚𑀸𑀥𑀺𑀭𑀸𑀚.notes: Highest imperial title in classical Sanskrit

𑀅𑀓𑁆𑀱𑀉𑀳𑀺𑀡𑀻.notes: Traditional division of army with all four wings

𑀘𑀢𑀼𑀭𑀗.notes: Army consisting of elephants, chariots, cavalry, infantry

𑀯𑀸𑀲𑁆𑀢𑀯𑁆𑀬.notes: Royal dwelling place and palace complex

𑀯𑀸𑀲𑁆𑀢𑀼𑀤𑁂𑀯.notes: Deity presiding over royal architectural structures

𑀦𑀻𑀢𑀺𑀲𑀸𑀭.notes: Core principles of royal governance and policy

𑀲𑀸𑀁𑀤𑀳𑀺𑀯𑀺𑀕𑁆𑀭𑀳.notes: Ministry dealing with foreign relations and warfare

𑀭𑀸𑀚𑀸𑀁𑀢𑀸𑀳𑀭𑀡.notes: Royal dining ceremonies and court banquets

𑀲𑀸𑀫𑀺𑀢𑁆𑀬.notes: Royal advisory council and deliberative assembly

𑀮𑁄𑀓𑀧𑀸𑀮𑀦.notes: Divine duty of kings to protect the world

𑀓𑀸𑀯𑁆𑀬𑀦𑀻𑀢𑀺.notes: Artistic approach to royal governance and policy

𑀭𑀸𑀚𑁆𑀢𑀦𑁆𑀢𑁆𑀭.notes: System and philosophy of monarchical rule

𑀤𑀻𑀦𑀸𑀭.notes: Standard gold currency in ancient Indian trade

𑀯𑀺𑀓𑁆𑀭𑀬.notes: Act of selling goods and merchandise

𑀓𑁆𑀭𑀬.notes: Act of purchasing and acquiring goods

𑀕𑁄𑀲𑁆𑀣.notes: Livestock trading post and cattle market

𑀯𑀸𑀲.notes: Permission to reside and trade in a territory

𑀦𑀸𑀯.notes: Trading vessel for river and coastal commerce
